science.title: Scientists discover the nutrient that can supercharge cellular energy
science.summary: Researchers discovered that leucine, a nutrient found in protein-rich foods, can supercharge mitochondria by protecting crucial energy-producing proteins inside cells. The breakthrough uncovers a powerful new link between diet and cellular energy — with possible implications for cancer and metabolic disease treatments.

science.title: Scientists Discover Genetic “Off Switch” That Supercharges CAR T Cells Against Cancer
science.summary: A new study reveals a possible way to make CAR T-cell therapy more durable and effective by targeting a single gene-regulating protein. CAR T-cell therapy is widely seen as a breakthrough in personalized cancer care. The treatment works by modifying a patient’s own immune cells so they can identify and attack cancer ce

science.title: Scientists create supercharged vitamin K that helps the brain heal itself
science.summary: Scientists in Japan have created powerful new vitamin K-based compounds that may help the brain regenerate lost neurons — a breakthrough that could one day change how diseases like Alzheimer’s and Parkinson’s are treated. science.title: 100-million-year-old bug had crab-like claws unlike any known insect
science.summary: Deep inside 100-million-year-old amber from Myanmar, scientists uncovered a bizarre ancient bug with clawed front legs that look more like a crab’s pincers than anything seen in modern insects. science.title: MIT scientists discover amino acid that helps the gut heal itself
science.summary: MIT scientists have identified cysteine — an amino acid found in foods like meat, dairy, beans, and nuts — as a potent trigger for intestinal repair. In mice, a cysteine-rich diet activated immune cells that released healing signals, helping stem cells rebuild damaged intestinal tissue after radiation exposure. Researc

tech.title: In more good news for Amazon, Snowflake signs $6B deal with AWS for AI CPU chips
tech.summary: Snowflake has signed a new, enormous five-year deal with Amazon to secure chips for AI usage. Nvidia is once again being put on notice.

tech.title: CrowdStrike and Google take down botnet used by hackers to target open source software developers
tech.summary: Cybercriminals used the Glassworm botnet to infect open source software projects with malware, and in turn hack the developers and companies that use that software.

tech.title: Huawei's ‘Chip Queen’ Throws Down the Gauntlet
tech.summary: The Chinese company is adapting to the demise of Moore’s Law, which guides chip production. It could complicate US chip dominance.

medicine.title: New drug could finally stop deadly fatty liver disease
medicine.summary: Scientists at UC San Diego have unveiled a potentially game-changing treatment for MASH, a severe fatty liver disease affecting millions worldwide. medicine.summary: A new analysis of clinical trial data led by Anisha P. Ganguly, MD, MPH, a general internist at UNC Health and member of UNC Lineberger Comprehensive Cancer Center, has proven that mailed fecal immunochemical tests can drastically increase colorectal cancer screening by 18% across multiple racial/ethnic groups. mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win AI for Math grants to accelerate mathematical discovery
mathematics.summary: Department of Mathematics researchers David Roe and Andrew Sutherland seek to advance automated theorem proving; four additional MIT alumni also awarded.

mathematics.title: Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up
mathematics.summary: By replacing the most fundamental concept in topology, Peter Scholze and Dustin Clausen are taking the first step in a far bigger program to understand why numbers behave the way they do. mathematics.title: What Do Gödel’s Incompleteness Theorems Truly Mean?

space.title: China shakes up its space programs to land astronauts on the moon by 2030: 'We will spare no effort'
space.summary: China is melding its robotic Chang'e lunar probe activities with the country's human spaceflight program in an effort to land astronauts on the moon by 2030.

space.title: FAA grounds SpaceX's Starship V3 megarocket after Flight 12 'mishap'
space.summary: The U.S. Federal Aviation Administration has declared the debut flight of SpaceX's Starship V3 vehicle a mishap and is requiring an investigation before the megarocket can fly again.

space.title: NASA will reveal the Artemis 3 astronauts on June 9
space.summary: On June 9, NASA will reveal the astronauts who will fly on the Artemis 3 docking mission in Earth orbit next year.

astronomy.title: How Did This Peculiar Planet Pair Form?
astronomy.summary: A planetary odd couple — a mini-Neptune and a hot Jupiter — probably formed much farther away from their star before migrating closer in. The post How Did This Peculiar Planet Pair Form? appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: This Week's Sky at a Glance, May 22 – 31
astronomy.summary: Venus and Jupiter — the Evening Star and the False Evening Star — draw closer together in the western twilight and point down to Mercury. The waxing Moon shows off its rich telescopic detail. The post This Week's Sky at a Glance, May 22 – 31 appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: NASA TESS Reveals Epic All-Sky Map of Distant Worlds
astronomy.summary: You’re on a camping trip with your family and your parents tell you to turn off all the lights. But, of course, your little brother wants to shine his flashlight directly at the sky saying aliens will see it. You finally get him to shut off his flashlight, and you give your eyes a few minutes to adjust to the darkness.

astronomy.summary: An international team led by Associate Professor Kimihiko Nakajima of Kanazawa University has captured a rare look at the early universe. architecture.title: Anatomy of a Maya City: The Urban Structure of Copán in Honduras
architecture.summary: Deep in western Honduras, within a valley near the Guatemalan border, lies the ancient Maya city of Copán. Flourishing during the Classic period between the fifth and ninth centuries CE, the city developed as a regional epicenter through trade networks, dynastic politics, and monumental architecture. architecture.title: When Architecture Moves: Kinetic Design and the Rituals of Space
architecture.summary: For centuries, architecture has been defined by unmoving permanence. A building is assumed to be fixed, its walls and foundation immobile in space. A growing number of architects are now challenging this assumption by incorporating movement into the very fabric and tectonic structures of buildings.

architecture.summary: The Centre Pompidou Hanwha designed by French architecture studio Wilmotte & Associés has been revealed ahead of its opening in Seoul, South Korea, next month.
